violations.finding: Hardcoded paths ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md used for command/agent reference checking with no parameter override. The script accepts PROJECT_ROOT as $1 but the commands/agents search path is unconditionally hardcoded to ~/.claude/ regardless of where the .claude directory actually lives.
violations.recommendation: Add a second parameter: CLAUDE_DIR="${2:-${CLAUDE_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/.claude}}". Use that variable for the grep paths: grep -rl "\b${name}\b" "$CLAUDE_DIR"/commands/*.md "$CLAUDE_DIR"/agents/*.md.

violations.finding: The ~/.claude/commands and ~/.claude/agents paths are not parameterized and cannot be overridden via CLI argument or environment variable. Same root cause as no-hardcoded-values finding above; recorded separately because it fails two distinct standards.
violations.recommendation: Same remediation as no-hardcoded-values: add CLAUDE_DIR parameter with PROJECT_ROOT-relative default fallback.

violations.finding: Final fallback branch name "master" is hardcoded with no CLI argument or environment variable override. The script's explicit purpose is dynamic branch discovery (rationale: hardcoded 'master' regressed in commit b5d447e), yet the fallback re-introduces the same hardcode. A repo whose default branch is neither 'main' nor 'master' (e.g. 'develop', 'trunk') will silently receive 'master' with no override path.
violations.recommendation: Accept the fallback via env var or positional arg: DEFAULT_BRANCH_FALLBACK="${1:-${DEFAULT_BRANCH_FALLBACK:-master}}" and assign DEFAULT_BRANCH="$DEFAULT_BRANCH_FALLBACK" when both discovery tiers fail. This preserves the existing default behavior while making the fallback overrideable.

standards_passed: merge-similar-scripts (detect-* scripts have distinct non-overlapping responsibilities)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified one missed critical violation (derive-default-branch.sh:23 hardcoded 'master' fallback), proposed a cleaner recommendation for the detect-dead-functions.sh CLAUDE_DIR default (use PROJECT_ROOT rather than $HOME), noted the two detect-dead-functions findings share a root cause, and flagged that those findings are pre-existing in a changed-files-only context (not applicable here — this is a full-scan audit).

findings.issue: MISSED_CRITICAL: scripts/derive-default-branch.sh:23 hardcodes 'master' as final fallback with no CLI/env override
findings.proposed_fix: Add finding: standard=parameterized-scripts, location=scripts/derive-default-branch.sh:23, finding=Final fallback branch name 'master' is hardcoded with no CLI/env override, recommendation=add FALLBACK_BRANCH env var or positional arg with 'master' as default
findings.rationale: Valid finding: the script's own rationale documents the danger of hardcoded branch names; the fallback reintroduces that exact pattern with no escape hatch.

findings.issue: INCOMPLETE_RECOMMENDATION: detect-dead-functions.sh CLAUDE_DIR default should reference PROJECT_ROOT rather than $HOME
findings.proposed_fix: Change recommendation default to CLAUDE_DIR="${2:-${CLAUDE_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/.claude}}" instead of ${HOME}/.claude
findings.rationale: PROJECT_ROOT-relative default is more consistent with the script's existing parameterization pattern and avoids assuming .claude always lives in $HOME.

findings.issue: DEDUP_OBSERVATION: Violations 1 and 2 for detect-dead-functions.sh share the same root cause (same line, same fix)
findings.proposed_fix: Keep two entries (one per standard) but note shared root cause in finding text
findings.rationale: The report tracks violations per-standard, not per-root-cause. Both standards 2 and 9 are independently violated. Keeping separate entries with a shared-root-cause note is the correct approach.

findings.issue: SCOPE_OBSERVATION: detect-dead-functions.sh:43 finding is pre-existing (git blame: commit 2ef201cb, 2026-02-16). In changed-files-only mode it would be advisory.
findings.proposed_fix: For full-scan mode (no --changed-files flag) keep as blocking. For changed-files mode classify as advisory_pre_existing unless line overlaps current diff hunk.
findings.rationale: This invocation has no --changed-files parameter, so this is a full-scan audit. The pre-existing finding remains blocking per full-scan rules.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Added derive-default-branch.sh:23 as a new violation (parameterized-scripts). Updated detect-dead-functions.sh recommendation to use PROJECT_ROOT-relative default instead of $HOME. Kept two separate violation entries for detect-dead-functions.sh:43 (standards 2 and 9) with shared-root-cause note.
